heading: Omitted
label: 2702 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection, , , , authorized appropriations for fiscal years 1968 and 1969.Pub. L. 90–222, § 281 Stat. 672Dec. 23, 1967

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–222, title IV, § 40181 Stat. 728Dec. 23, 1967“The amendments made by this Act [see Short Title of 1967 Amendment note set out under ] shall be in effect immediately upon its enactment [], except as provided in this section. Until , the provisions of section 202 of the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964 as in effect immediately prior to the enactment of this Act [] shall apply to community action agencies in existence and funded prior to the enactment of this Act [], except that in any grant or funding agreement made with such an agency prior to , adequate provision shall be made for transfer of functions, obligations, records, authority, and funds to any community action agency designated pursuant to sections 210 or 211 of the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964 as amended by this Act [sections 2790 or 2791 of this title]: , That nothing in this Act shall require the termination before of an existing community action agency or any program assisted under the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964 [this chapter] prior to the designation of, and provision of financial assistance to, a community action agency or other agency established under sections 210 and 211 of the Economic Opportunity Act [sections 2790 and 2791 of this title] as amended by this Act.”section 2701 of this titlesection 2782 of this titleDec. 23, 1967June 30, 1968Dec. 23, 1967June 30, 1968February 1, 1969Provided, however

Access of GAO to Grantee’s Records, , , provided in part that all grant agreements were to provide that the General Accounting Office would have access to the records bearing exclusively upon the Federal grant.Pub. L. 91–667, title III84 Stat. 2018Jan. 1, 1971Similar provisions were contained in , , .Pub. L. 91–204, title III84 Stat. 46Mar. 5, 1970

Investigation and Evaluation of Economic Opportunity Programs by the Comptroller General; Submission of Final Report byDecember 1, 1968, , , authorized the Comptroller General of the United States to make an investigation in sufficient depth of programs and activities financed in whole or in part by funds authorized under this section in order to determine the efficiency of the administration of such programs and activities by the Office of Economic Opportunity and by local public and private agencies carrying out such programs and activities, and the extent to which such programs and activities achieve the objectives set forth in the relevant part or title of this chapter which authorizes such programs or activities, and to transmit his final report to the Congress not later than containing a detailed statement of his findings and conclusions together with such recommendations, including recommendations for additional legislation as he deemed advisable.Pub. L. 90–222, title II81 Stat. 727Dec. 23, 1967Dec. 1, 1968
